senses.definition: the property of having pulses or pulsation, especially regular variation in strength or volume over time
examples: The pulsatility of the arterial signal helped doctors assess heart function.
Ultrasound imaging examined the pulsatility of the blood flow.

senses.definition: a genus of flowering plants in the buttercup family, commonly known as pasqueflowers
examples: The garden included several species of pulsatilla in spring bloom.
Herbalists sometimes reference pulsatilla in traditional remedies.

senses.definition: plural form of pulsatilla; multiple pulsatilla plants
examples: The meadow was dotted with several pulsatillas.
Collectors studied the different pulsatillas present in the habitat.

senses.definition: throbbing or beating repeatedly with regular rhythm
examples: The neon sign had a pulsating glow.
He could feel a pulsating pain in his temple.

senses.definition: the act or process of pulsating; a rhythmic throbbing or beating
examples: The pulsation of the heart was visible on the monitor.
They measured the pulsation rate during exercise.

senses.definition: relating to or caused by pulsation; showing periodic expansion and contraction
examples: Pulsational forces influenced the vibration of the string.
The instrument tracked pulsational changes in pressure.

senses.definition: plural of pulsation; multiple rhythmic throbs or beats
examples: The machine emitted faint pulsations.
Monitoring the pulsations helped diagnose the issue.

senses.definition: relating to or characterized by pulsation or a pulsing rhythm
examples: The device produced a pulsative display on the screen.
Pulsative pressure changes were recorded.

senses.definition: in a pulsing or throbbing manner
examples: The indicator blinked pulsatively as the system warmed up.
The beat changed pulsatively with the tempo.

senses.definition: a device or organism that pulsates; a component that produces pulses
examples: The heart is a natural pulsator.
The experimental circuit used a pulsator to generate pulses.

senses.definition: plural of pulsator; multiple pulse-generating devices or organisms
examples: The lab used several pulsators in the experiment.
Medical devices can be categorized by the type of pulsator they use.

senses.definition: of or relating to pulsation; characterized by a beating or throbbing rhythm
examples: The pulsatory motion of the fan cooled the device.
Scientists studied the pulsatory cycle of the heart.

senses.definition: the regular beating of the heart or a finger check of heart rate; a single beat in a rhythm
examples: The doctor checked my pulse.
The clock's pulse kept a steady tempo.
Her pulse raced with excitement.

senses.definition: a single short burst or beat in a rhythm or signal
examples: The drum produced a sharp pulse.
The music has a strong backbeat and a clear pulse.

senses.definition: to measure the heart rate by feeling the pulse or to cause a heartbeat rhythm
examples: Paramedics pulsed the artery to check circulation.
The engine pulses with energy as it accelerates.

senses.definition: the rhythm or tempo of a pulse or heartbeat, often used in music or medical contexts
examples: The musician adjusted the tempo to match the drum pulsebeat.
Doctors monitored the pulsebeat to assess stability.

senses.definition: having pulses or pulses; emitting pulses
examples: The indicator screen showed a pulsed output.
A pulsed laser emits light in short bursts.

senses.definition: a type of jet engine that uses intermittent combustion to produce thrust
examples: The old aircraft used pulsejet propulsion.
Pulsejets were popular in early jet-powered designs.

senses.definition: plural of pulsejet; multiple pulsejet engines
examples: The model aircraft used several pulsejets.
Historical aircraft experiments included pulsejets.

senses.definition: without a pulse; lacking a detectable heartbeat or rhythm
examples: The patient remained pulseless despite resuscitation efforts.
The machine reported a pulseless signal.

senses.definition: in a pulseless manner; without a detectable pulse
examples: The monitor continued to beat pulselessly until help arrived.
The movie depicted the city pulselessly silent after the blackout.

senses.definition: the state of having no detectable pulse; absence of heartbeat
examples: Pulselessness indicated that the patient required immediate intervention.
In the clinic, pulselessness persisted despite treatment.

senses.definition: plural of pulselessness; multiple instances of absence of pulse
examples: Cases of pulselessnesses were recorded in the emergency ward.
Researchers studied variations in pulselessnesses across patients.

senses.definition: resembling a pulse or having a pulse-like quality
examples: The glow from the screen was pulselike in the dark room.
The shadows moved pulselike with the tree branches.

senses.definition: an anatomical term referring to a small, rounded part or structure; very uncommon in modern usage
examples: Ancient texts mention the pulellum in anatomical descriptions.

senses.definition: a device or person that pulses or causes pulses
examples: The device acts as a pulser to generate electrical pulses.
A human pulser could refer to an athlete with a strong heartbeat rhythm.

senses.definition: plural of pulser; multiple pulse-generating devices or people
examples: The lab used several pulsers in the circuit.
Clinical equipment included two pulsers for calibration.

senses.definition: relating to or characterized by pulsation; having a pulsing rhythm
examples: The pulsatory heartbeat was monitored closely.
A pulsatory signal enabled the device to sync with the system.

senses.definition: to cause to beat with a regular rhythm; to send out pulses
examples: The device pules the signal to the receiver.
The heart will pulse faster during exercise.

senses.definition: see above for pulsejet; repeated to satisfy structure
see above for pulsejets; repeated to satisfy structure
without a pulse; not having a heartbeat
examples: The victim was found pulseless and needed urgent care.
A pulseless monitor indicated a critical situation.

senses.definition: in a pulseless manner; without a detectable pulse
examples: The monitor displayed the heart rate pulselessly.
The town lay pulselessly quiet after the blackout.

senses.definition: the state of not having a pulse; absence of heartbeat
examples: She feared pulselessness during the medical emergency.
Pulselessness is a medical emergency.

senses.definition: plural of pulselessness; multiple instances of no pulse
examples: The report documented several pulselessnesses across patients.
Differences in pulselessnesses were analyzed by the team.

senses.definition: resembling or suggesting a pulse
examples: The glow had a pulselike shimmer.
A pulselike tremor ran through the device.

senses.definition: an anatomical term related to small rounded structures; rare usage
examples: Classical texts mention the pulsellum as a component in anatomy.

senses.definition: a device or person that pulses; a creator of pulses
examples: The device acts as a pulser to generate pulses.
Some medical devices use a pulser to regulate therapy.

senses.definition: plural of pulser; multiple pulse-generating devices or persons
examples: The lab used several pulsers in the circuit.
Medical devices relied on accurate pulsers.
